versions.tailored_for: Single-column, keyword-forward structure for reliable ATS parsing and JD keyword matching (aligned to the Razorpay Risk & Fraud JD).
versions.formatting_notes: Single column, standard section headings, plain hyphens instead of em/en dashes, no tables or graphics. Skills moved directly below Summary for early keyword density.
key_changes: Moved Skills section from last to second position
Replaced em dash (—) and en dash (–) characters with commas and plain hyphens
Replaced 'Responsible for' and 'Helped' openers with active verbs (Drove, Improved) without changing claimed scope
versions.full_text:
Ananya Sharma
Bengaluru, India | ananya.sharma.pm@gmail.com | +91-98765-43210 | linkedin.com/in/ananyasharma-pm

SUMMARY
Senior Product Manager with 8 years of experience across fintech and consumer marketplaces, including 4 years owning risk, fraud, and compliance-adjacent products. Skilled in product strategy, roadmapping, SQL, A/B testing, and stakeholder management.

SKILLS
Product Strategy, Roadmapping, SQL, A/B Testing, Stakeholder Management, User Research, RBI Compliance Basics, Payments Domain Knowledge, Figma, JIRA, Amplitude

WORK EXPERIENCE

Senior Product Manager, Cashfree Payments, Bengaluru
June 2022 - Present
- Owned the merchant onboarding product line for Cashfree's payment gateway
- Led a cross-functional team of 6 (engineering, design, data) to launch a revamped KYC flow, reducing merchant onboarding time from 3 days to 18 hours
- Drove the roadmap for the risk and fraud detection product area
- Worked closely with compliance and legal teams on RBI regulatory requirements
- Launched a self-serve merchant dashboard that increased weekly active merchants by 22%
- Partnered with data science to introduce a fraud scoring model, cutting chargeback rate by 31%

Product Manager, Swiggy, Bengaluru
July 2020 - May 2022
- Managed the restaurant partner rating and feedback product
- Improved partner satisfaction scores
- Drove A/B experiments on the delivery partner app, improving order acceptance rate by 9%
- Coordinated with 4 engineering pods across two cities to ship a partner payout redesign
- Authored PRDs and prioritized the backlog for the partner tools squad

Associate Product Manager, Zomato, Gurugram
May 2018 - June 2020
- Supported the search and discovery team
- Partnered with senior PMs on user research and competitive analysis
- Contributed to a redesign of the restaurant listing page

PROJECTS

Campus Meal-Split App (Side Project)
- Built an expense-splitting app for hostel meal groups using React Native and Firebase
- Reached approximately 300 students at IIT Delhi during a 6-month pilot

EDUCATION
B.Tech, Computer Science, Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Delhi, 2018

versions.tailored_for: Optimized for a ~30-second recruiter scan — leads with quantified achievements before process detail.
versions.formatting_notes: Adds a 4-line Highlights block up top; reorders bullets within each role to lead with metrics; compresses the Zomato entry to its two strongest lines.
key_changes: Added a Highlights section surfacing the 4 strongest quantified results
Reordered Cashfree and Swiggy bullets to lead with metrics, not process description
Compressed Zomato from 3 bullets to 2 without dropping any factual content

Ananya Sharma
Senior Product Manager — Payments & Risk | Bengaluru, India
ananya.sharma.pm@gmail.com | +91-98765-43210 | linkedin.com/in/ananyasharma-pm

SUMMARY
Senior PM who cut chargebacks 31% and slashed merchant onboarding time from 3 days to 18 hours at Cashfree Payments. 8 years building product across fintech and consumer marketplaces (Cashfree, Swiggy, Zomato), including 4 years owning risk, fraud, and compliance-adjacent products.

HIGHLIGHTS
- Cut chargeback rate 31% by partnering with data science on a fraud scoring model
- Reduced merchant onboarding time from 3 days to 18 hours, leading a 6-person KYC flow redesign
- Grew weekly active merchants 22% with a new self-serve merchant dashboard
- Improved delivery-partner order acceptance rate 9% through A/B experimentation at Swiggy

EXPERIENCE

Senior Product Manager, Cashfree Payments — Jun 2022-Present
- Cut chargeback rate 31% by partnering with data science to introduce a fraud scoring model
- Reduced merchant onboarding time from 3 days to 18 hours, leading a 6-person cross-functional team through a KYC flow redesign
- Grew weekly active merchants 22% by launching a self-serve merchant dashboard
- Owned the merchant onboarding product line and the risk and fraud detection roadmap
- Worked directly with compliance and legal on RBI regulatory requirements

Product Manager, Swiggy — Jul 2020-May 2022
- Improved delivery-partner order acceptance rate 9% by driving A/B experiments
- Coordinated 4 engineering pods across two cities to ship a partner payout redesign
- Managed the restaurant partner rating and feedback product; improved partner satisfaction scores
- Authored PRDs and prioritized the backlog for the partner tools squad

Associate Product Manager, Zomato — May 2018-Jun 2020
- Partnered with senior PMs on user research and competitive analysis for the search and discovery team
- Contributed to a redesign of the restaurant listing page

SIDE PROJECT: Campus Meal-Split App — React Native + Firebase app reaching ~300 students at IIT Delhi (6-month pilot)

SKILLS: Product Strategy - Roadmapping - SQL - A/B Testing - Stakeholder Management - RBI Compliance Basics - Payments Domain Knowledge

EDUCATION: B.Tech, Computer Science, IIT Delhi, 2018

versions.tailored_for: Emphasizes ownership, breadth, and comfort with ambiguity for a startup audience.
versions.formatting_notes: Title genericized to 'Product Manager' in the header for broader startup-role fit; summary and bullets foreground ownership language ('Owned', 'Drove') over process language.
key_changes: Summary rewritten to foreground end-to-end ownership and the side project
Verbs shifted toward ownership framing ('Owned', 'Drove', 'Ran') where the underlying scope supports it
Side project given equal visual weight to full-time roles, since it demonstrates hands-on building

versions.skip_reason: The source resume's highest title is 'Senior Product Manager' with no VP/Director-level title, no stated P&L or organizational ownership, and no evidence of direct reports (team leadership is scoped to a 6-person cross-functional project team and coordination with 4 engineering pods, not people management). An executive-tier rewrite would need to imply org-level scope the source doesn't support, which would misrepresent the candidate rather than just reframe them.

global_changes_summary: Replaced weak/passive openers ('Responsible for', 'Helped', 'Worked on') with active verbs across all versions without changing claimed scope
Reordered bullets within roles differently per version (chronological/process order for ATS and enterprise, achievement-first for recruiter and startup)
Preserved every factual claim (employers, titles, dates, team sizes, metrics) unchanged across all four applicable versions
Consistently declined to add scope, metrics, or claims (e.g. PM mentoring, SQL usage specifics) the source resume doesn't support — see unmet_improvement_opportunities

unmet_improvement_opportunities.reason_not_improved: The source states satisfaction scores improved but gives no baseline or resulting number; a specific percentage could not be added without inventing one.

unmet_improvement_opportunities.location: Cashfree — 'Owned the merchant onboarding product line' bullet
unmet_improvement_opportunities.reason_not_improved: No scale indicator (merchant volume, transaction value) is stated in the source, so this bullet could not be strengthened with a scope metric without fabricating one.
SQL is listed as a skill but the source never ties it to a specific action or outcome, so no demonstrating bullet could be written without inventing a scenario.
No version claims PM mentoring or management experience, since the source resume contains no evidence of it; this cannot be addressed by rewriting.

human_readable_summary:
## Resume Rewrite: Ananya Sharma

**4 of 5 versions generated.** ATS, Recruiter, Startup, and Enterprise versions are ready to use. The **Executive version was deliberately not generated** — the source resume's scope (Senior PM, 6-person project team, no stated direct reports) doesn't support an executive-tier framing without overstating it, so it's marked `applicable: false` with a specific reason rather than force-generated.

**What changed, and what didn't:** every version keeps the same employers, titles, dates, team sizes, and metrics — only tone, ordering, verb choice, and section emphasis differ per audience. Four specific opportunities to add metrics or specifics were identified and deliberately left alone rather than invented (see Unmet Improvement Opportunities) — most notably, SQL is a listed skill with no demonstrating bullet in the source, and no version claims PM mentoring experience since none exists in the source.

**Fastest win if the candidate wants to close a gap for real:** get a concrete number for the Swiggy partner-satisfaction improvement and a scope indicator (merchant volume, GMV) for the Cashfree onboarding ownership bullet — both would meaningfully strengthen every version once available.
limitations: The executive version was not generated because the source resume's title and scope don't support an executive-tier framing without overstating it — see that version's skip_reason.
Several bullets that could be stronger with a metric were left qualitative rather than invented — see unmet_improvement_opportunities.
Formatting is described in prose (formatting_notes) since output is plain text; actual visual layout (fonts, spacing, columns) needs to be applied when the candidate transfers this into a document.
